steps.title: Cancel the matching term
steps.content: Set the expanded forms equal: $$x^2+5x+6=x^2-5x+16.$$ The $x^2$ terms cancel, leaving $$5x+6=-5x+16.$$

steps.title: Solve and verify
steps.content: Add $5x$ and subtract $6$: $$10x=10,$$ so $x=1$. Graphing the two sides also shows one intersection at $x=1$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The solution $x=1$ matches choice A. The other listed values do not make the two sides equal when substituted into the original equation.
